name: FIRST SOLAR, INC.
business: First Solar designs, manufactures, and sells cadmium telluride (CdTe) thin-film photovoltaic solar modules. The company primarily sells to system developers, independent power producers, utilities, commercial and industrial companies, large corporate energy buyers, and other system owners and operators. The majority of its modules are sold to customers with projects in the United States.

exposures.sentence: Further, overall electric load growth, especially as a result of artificial intelligence (AI)-driven data center demand, continues to increase.

Other technological developments in the renewable energy industry, such as the advancement of energy storage capabilities, have further enhanced the prospects of solar energy as an attractive complement to traditional forms of energy generation.
China announced that it would tighten export controls for five key minerals, including products containing tellurium, which is one of the main components of our CdTe modules.
We continue to research and develop our thin-film semiconductor technology, with a focus on the use of perovskite thin films.

Our CuRe program is intended to improve our current semiconductor structure by replacing copper with certain other elements that are expected to enhance module performance by improving its bifaciality characteristics, improving its temperature coefficient, and improving its warranted degradation.
Our customers include system developers, independent power producers, utilities, commercial and industrial companies, large corporate energy buyers, and other system owners and operators.
We are the world's largest thin film PV solar module manufacturer and the largest PV solar module manufacturer in the Western Hemisphere.
China announced that it would tighten export controls for five key minerals, including products containing tellurium, which is one of the main components of our CdTe modules.

catalysts.event: Conversion of one Ohio facility to CuRe technology
catalysts.sentence: Beginning in the first quarter of 2026, we intend to permanently convert one of our Ohio facilities to CuRe, followed by a phased replication of the technology across certain manufacturing facilities within our fleet.

catalysts.event: Commencement of operations at sixth U.S. manufacturing facility
catalysts.sentence: We are in the process of further expanding our domestic manufacturing capacity, including the construction of our sixth U.S. manufacturing facility to onshore final production processes for modules initiated by our international fleet, which is expected to commence operations in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: The U.S. has an installed solar generation capacity of approximately 266 GW.
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. currently has an installed solar generation capacity of approximately 266 GW, and, in 2025 alone, the U.S. installed over 30 GW of utility-scale solar capacity.

tamClaims.claim: In 2025, the U.S. installed over 30 GW of utility-scale solar capacity.
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. currently has an installed solar generation capacity of approximately 266 GW, and, in 2025 alone, the U.S. installed over 30 GW of utility-scale solar capacity.

tamClaims.claim: India has an installed solar generation capacity of approximately 132 GW.
tamClaims.sentence: India represents the third largest global market for PV solar energy with an installed solar generation capacity of approximately 132 GW.

tamClaims.claim: Projected installed solar energy generation capacity in India of approximately 280 GW by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on these targets, it is projected that the installed solar energy generation capacity will be approximately 280 GW by 2030.
